title: pheno-port-adapter — canonical port adapter trait + opaque Connection + AdapterError (thiserror)
author: forge
summary: Authored the pheno-port-adapter standalone crate at pheno-port-adapter/ with the canonical PortAdapter trait, an opaque Connection handle, and a thiserror-derived AdapterError enum. PortAdapter is Send + Sync with four sync methods: name(&self) -> &str, health(&self) -> Result<(), AdapterError>, connect(&self, endpoint: &str) -> Result<Connection, AdapterError>, and disconnect(&self) -> Result<(), AdapterError>. Connection is an opaque handle with a private id field. AdapterError has four variants: ConnectFailed, DisconnectFailed, HealthCheckFailed, and Timeout. The crate is a standalone package (empty [workspace] in its own Cargo.toml), matching the L3 convention. All 5 spec-named tests pass, and clippy is clean.

public_api.pheno_port_adapter::PortAdapter: pub trait PortAdapter: Send + Sync { fn name(&self) -> &str; fn health(&self) -> Result<(), AdapterError>; fn connect(&self, endpoint: &str) -> Result<Connection, AdapterError>; fn disconnect(&self) -> Result<(), AdapterError>; } — canonical port adapter trait for any transport adapter (TCP, Unix socket, WebSocket, gRPC, etc.)
public_api.pheno_port_adapter::Connection: pub struct Connection { pub(crate) id: String } — opaque handle representing an active connection; Debug derive provided for diagnostics
public_api.pheno_port_adapter::AdapterError: thiserror enum { ConnectFailed(String), DisconnectFailed(String), HealthCheckFailed(String), Timeout } — canonical error type for all adapter operations; Send + Sync + 'static

test_coverage: pheno-port-adapter/src/lib.rs: connect_returns_connection (canonical spec name, line 70) — mock adapter connects to a valid endpoint and returns a Connection with the expected id
pheno-port-adapter/src/lib.rs: disconnect_returns_ok (canonical spec name, line 80) — mock adapter disconnect returns Ok(())
pheno-port-adapter/src/lib.rs: health_check_passes (canonical spec name, line 90) — healthy mock adapter returns Ok(()) from health()
pheno-port-adapter/src/lib.rs: connect_to_invalid_endpoint_fails (canonical spec name, line 100) — mock adapter rejects an invalid endpoint with AdapterError::ConnectFailed
pheno-port-adapter/src/lib.rs: adapter_name_is_non_empty (canonical spec name, line 111) — mock adapter name returns a non-empty string

verification.cargo_test: 5/5 pass via cargo test -p pheno-port-adapter from the crate directory
verification.cargo_clippy: clean (no warnings, -D warnings applied) via cargo clippy -p pheno-port-adapter --all-targets -- -D warnings from the crate directory

spec_alignment: The L4 #66 spec called for a standalone empty [workspace] Cargo.toml, a pub trait PortAdapter: Send + Sync with four sync methods, a pub struct Connection opaque handle, a pub enum AdapterError (thiserror) with ConnectFailed, DisconnectFailed, HealthCheckFailed, and Timeout variants, and >=5 named tests. All deliverables are present verbatim: connect_returns_connection, disconnect_returns_ok, health_check_passes, connect_to_invalid_endpoint_fails, adapter_name_is_non_empty.
spec_deviations: None. All spec deliverables are present and verified.
